title: citrus × aurantium var. bergamia
intro: Citrus × aurantium var. bergamia, also known as bergamot, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family and a variety of the sour orange tree. The medicinal properties of bergamot include its high content of limonene, a compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of bergamot include reducing stress and anxiety, improving mood, and lowering blood pressure, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Bergamot is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tea, essential oil, and supplement capsules, which can be used for aromatherapy, skincare, and digestive health support.
